approx_date: Initial allegations/investigation began ~2017; restated financials for fiscal years 2012-2017 filed around 2018-2019 (delayed 10-K filed late 2019); CEO/founder Parker H. "Pete" Petit and other executives departed around mid-2018.
event_description: MiMedx Group, Inc. (MDXG) was involved in a well-publicized accounting scandal. Starting around late 2017/early 2018, whistleblowers and short-seller reports alleged improper revenue recognition practices, including channel-stuffing and pressuring distributors to accept product they hadn't ordered, in order to inflate reported sales. The company's Audit Committee launched an internal investigation, and the SEC and DOJ opened formal investigations into the company's accounting practices. As a result, MiMedx announced it could no longer rely on previously issued financial statements for fiscal years 2012 through 2017 (an Item 4.02 non-reliance disclosure), delayed multiple periodic SEC filings, and was at risk of Nasdaq delisting for failure to timely file. Founder and CEO Parker H. "Pete" Petit, along with COO William Taylor and other senior executives, resigned/were terminated in mid-2018 amid the scandal. The company subsequently filed restated financial statements (a multi-year restatement) and became current with its SEC filings around late 2019. The SEC later brought enforcement actions (I recall settlements/charges against former executives, including Petit) related to the improper revenue recognition scheme, though I am less certain of the exact final SEC settlement dates/terms.
